data.title: Bitcoin underperformance signals ‘distinct’ Q3 altseason: Grayscale
data.description:
Asset management company Grayscale has suggested that the third quarter of 2025 may have represented an altcoin season “distinct from those in the past,” based in part on the underperformance of Bitcoin and a boost from centralized exchanges.

According to a Grayscale report released on Thursday, though returns across crypto-related markets, including Bitcoin , Ether , AI, and smart contracts, were positive in Q3, the quarter may have stood out as an “alt season.” The asset manager said the smart contracts sector benefited from stablecoin legislation — likely referring to the GENIUS Act signed into law in the US in July — while AI, currencies and BTC lagged behind.

“Bitcoin underperformed other market segments, and the pattern of returns could be considered a crypto ‘alt season’ — although distinct from other periods of falling Bitcoin dominance in the past,” said the Grayscale report.

Among other themes in the report were a surge in the number of crypto treasuries holding a variety of tokens on their balance sheets, greater adoption of stablecoins in the US and rising volume in centralized exchanges.

Grayscale speculated that other US policies, including a digital asset market structure bill pending in Congress, could help drive crypto markets in the fourth quarter of 2025.

Related: Crypto markets are down, but corporate proxies are doing far worse

Though the price of BTC increased significantly in Q3, reaching an all-time high of more than $120,000 in August, its performance was still lagging when compared to other assets. Research suggested that Bitcoin and altcoins were falling behind gold and stocks in reaching new all-time high prices, in part due to stablecoins leaving exchanges.

Optimism for crypto exchange-traded funds

As one of the largest asset managers offering cryptocurrency exchange-traded funds (ETFs), Grayscale has been a first mover in digital asset investment vehicles.

The company reported that the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) recently approving new listing standards for crypto ETFs could also help drive markets in Q4. The US regulator has already signed off on one of its multi-asset crypto exchange-traded products offering exposure to BTC, ETH, XRP (XRP), Solana (SOL) and Cardano (ADA).

data.title: SoftBank, Ark Invest Among Potential Investors In Tether’s $15 Billion Funding Round
data.description:
The industry’s largest stablecoin issuer, Tether (USDT), is reportedly in discussions with a series of leading firms including SoftBank Group and Ark Investment Management, for a significant funding round aimed at raising between $15 billion and $20 billion. 

This capital influx could potentially value the company at an astonishing $500 billion. Bloomberg News first reported these developments, indicating that Tether is exploring private placement opportunities to solidify its position in the market.

SoftBank And Ark Invest’s Potential Involvement

Per the report, the involvement of SoftBank and Ark could significantly enhance Tether’s credibility in the eyes of mainstream investors, particularly as the company seeks to overcome previous scrutiny regarding its role in the cryptocurrency ecosystem.

Amidst this search for funding, Tether is also expanding its investment horizons beyond digital assets, venturing into sectors such as artificial intelligence (AI), telecommunications, cloud computing, and real estate. 

Adding to the momentum, Tether recently appointed Bo Hines, a former advisor to President Trump on cryptocurrency matters, as CEO of its US division. 

This move aligns with Tether’s vision to establish a new operation in the US, adhering to the new regulatory environment, particularly following the introduction of a new dollar-pegged cryptocurrency aimed at businesses and institutions, dubbed “USAT.” 

Tether And US Regulatory Standards

As NewsBTC reported recently, the new token adheres to the regulatory framework established by the GENIUS Act, the first stablecoin legislation signed into law by President Trump, highlighting Tether’s focus on aligning with US regulatory standards.

Paolo Ardoino, Tether’s CEO, noted that the firm’s USDT stablecoin serves as a crucial financial tool for millions in emerging markets, showcasing how digital assets can foster trust, resilience, and financial freedom on a global scale.

Featured image from DALL-E, chart from TradingView.com

data.title: Trump to Slap New Tariffs on Pharma, Big Trucks — WSJ
data.description:
By Joseph Pisani, Liz Essley Whyte and Bob Tita

WASHINGTON — President Trump announced many new tariffs Thursday, including a large one on drugs from pharmaceutical companies that aren't building plants in the U.S.

"Starting October 1st, 2025, we will be imposing a 100% Tariff on any branded or patented Pharmaceutical Product, unless a Company IS BUILDING their Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Plant in America," Trump posted on his social-media site, Truth Social, without providing details.

Many of the largest pharmaceutical companies have announced new construction in the U.S. in recent months including Eli Lilly, AstraZeneca, Roche Holding and GSK. So far this year, more than a dozen drugmakers have pledged to spend more than $350 billion collectively by the end of this decade on manufacturing, research and development, and other functions in the U.S., The Wall Street Journal has reported.

Other tariffs Trump announced Thursday include levies on large trucks and home goods. All of them will begin Oct. 1, he said.

Roughly nine out of 10 prescriptions in the U.S. are filled with generic drugs, which wouldn't be affected by a tariff on "branded or patented" products, as Trump outlined. This summer the Trump administration placed a 15% tariff on most pharmaceuticals coming from the European Union.

Pharmaceutical companies have been fearful of tariffs because many have manufacturing plants in Europe and other regions outside the U.S.

Drugmakers oppose new duties, arguing that medicines have historically been exempt from tariffs. They have lobbied the administration to spare pharmaceuticals, saying a better way to spur U.S. drug manufacturing is through tax policy.

"Most innovative medicines prescribed in America are already made in America," said Alex Schriver, a spokesman for Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America. "PhRMA companies continue to announce hundreds of billions in new U.S. investments....Tariffs risk those plans because every dollar spent on tariffs is a dollar that cannot be invested in American manufacturing or the development of future treatments and cures," he added.

Heavy trucks brought from outside the U.S. will be hit with a 25% tariff, Trump said, adding that it was "to protect our Great Heavy Truck Manufacturers from unfair outside competition."

Commercial truck manufacturers rely on plants in Mexico to produce heavy-duty trucks for the U.S. market. Germany's Daimler Truck North America unit assembles some Freightliner-brand trucks in Mexico. Freightliner is the North America market leader in heavy-duty trucks, with about a 40% share of the market. Germany's Traton Group produces International-brand heavy-duty trucks in Mexico.

"There will be more upward price pressure from tariffs, especially on the ones manufactured in Mexico," said Kenny Vieth, a truck-market analyst in Indiana.

Kitchen cabinets, bathroom vanities and other similar products will get a 50% tariff, Trump said. A 30% tariff will be imposed on upholstered furniture.

"The reason for this is the large-scale "FLOODING" of these products into the United States by other outside Countries," Trump wrote. "It is a very unfair practice, but we must protect, for National Security and other reasons, our Manufacturing process."

Overall, the markets took the tariff news in stride, with European indexes and U.S. stock futures up slightly early Friday. Shares of furniture retailers such as Wayfair, and Restoration Hardware owner RH, slipped in premarket trading. Shares in Daimler Truck, maker of the Freightliner brand, fell 2%. Some Japanese and Korean drug maker stocks fell slightly, though shares of the major drug makers in Europe that have committed to investing in the U.S. were little changed.

The Supreme Court agreed this month to hear arguments in November on the Trump administration's bid to save its sweeping global tariffs, setting the stage for a final ruling on a cornerstone of the president's economic agenda. The tariffs, which were dealt a string of defeats in the lower courts, are set to remain in place until the case is decided.

Write to Joseph Pisani at joseph.pisani@wsj.com, Liz Essley Whyte at liz.whyte@wsj.com and Bob Tita at robert.tita@wsj.com

data.title: National Healthcare Corp.: Oper Chierf R. Michael Ussery to Retire From Co. Dec. 31 >NHC
Marathon Bancorp, Inc. /MD/ SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Marathon Bancorp, Inc., a Maryland corporation and the mid-tier holding company for Marathon Bank, has released its annual 10-K report, detailing significant financial and operational achievements for the fiscal year. The report highlights the company's strategic initiatives, business performance, and the challenges it faces in a competitive market environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $9.6 million, primarily due to an increase in other interest income offset by a decrease in debt securities income.
  • Net Interest Income: $6.0 million, an increase of $203,000 or 3.8% from the previous year, driven by an increase in net interest-earning assets.
  • Net Income: $42,000, an increase of $229,000 or 122.7% from a net loss of $187,000 in the previous year, primarily due to an increase in net interest income and a decrease in non-interest expenses.
  • Net Income Per Share - Basic: $0.02, compared to a loss of $0.07 in the previous year, reflecting improved profitability.
  • Net Income Per Share - Diluted: $0.02, compared to a loss of $0.07 in the previous year, indicating consistent earnings per share improvement.

Business Highlights

  • Business Structure: Marathon Bancorp, Inc. was formed in December 2020 and serves as the mid-tier holding company for Marathon Bank. The Bank completed its reorganization into a mutual holding company structure in April 2021 and transitioned to a stock holding company form in April 2025.
  • Market Area and Expansion: Marathon Bank operates in Wisconsin, with its main office in Wausau and four branch offices in Marathon, Ozaukee, and Waukesha Counties. The Bank has expanded into Southeastern Wisconsin, including the Milwaukee metropolitan area, with new branches in Brookfield and Mequon.
  • Product and Service Offerings: Marathon Bank offers a variety of financial products and services, including commercial and multifamily real estate loans, one- to four-family residential real estate loans, commercial and industrial loans, construction loans, and consumer loans. The Bank also provides deposit products such as checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit.
  • Loan Portfolio Composition: As of June 30, 2025, the Bank's loan portfolio primarily consists of commercial real estate loans (45.4%), one- to four-family residential real estate loans (27.8%), and multifamily real estate loans (23.6%).
  • Geographical Performance: Marathon County's population grew by 4.37% since 2010, with significant employment in education, healthcare, and manufacturing. Ozaukee County saw an 8.44% population increase, while Milwaukee County experienced a 3.93% decrease. Waukesha County's population grew by 6.97%, with the highest median household income among the Bank's market areas.
  • Competition: Marathon Bank faces competition from large money center and regional banks, community banks, credit unions, and fintech companies. As of June 30, 2025, the Bank held a 2.99% market share of deposits in Marathon County.
  • Lending Activities: The Bank focuses on originating commercial real estate and multifamily real estate loans to increase yield and manage interest rate risk. It also sells fixed-rate conforming one- to four-family residential real estate loans while holding adjustable-rate loans in its portfolio.
  • New Branch Openings: In 2024, Marathon Bank opened a new branch in Brookfield, Wisconsin, as part of its strategy to expand into Southeastern Wisconsin.
  • Future Outlook: Marathon Bank plans to continue focusing on commercial real estate and multifamily real estate loans to increase overall yield and manage interest rate risk, while remaining committed to being a significant residential mortgage lender in its market areas.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Conversion to Stock Holding Company: Marathon Bancorp completed its conversion from a mutual holding company to a stock holding company, generating gross proceeds of $16.9 million. This strategic move was aimed at enhancing the company's capital structure and market presence. The conversion included the sale of 1,693,411 shares of common stock, which involved a public offering and an allocation to the Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP). The company also established a liquidation account to benefit eligible account holders post-conversion.
  • Capital Management: The company focused on strengthening its capital base by raising $15.2 million net proceeds from the conversion offering. It also provided a term loan to the ESOP to finance the purchase of shares, consolidating existing loans into a new $2.1 million term loan. Additionally, Marathon Bancorp repurchased and retired common stock shares, and adjusted existing stock options and restricted stock awards based on a 1.3728-to-1 exchange ratio. The company maintained a strong liquidity position with an $82.8 million line of credit from the Federal Home Loan Bank of Chicago and $17.5 million available from the Federal Reserve Bank.
  • Future Outlook: Marathon Bancorp plans to maintain a strong liquidity position and anticipates sufficient funds to meet current funding commitments. The company expects to retain a substantial portion of maturing time deposits and is prepared to utilize Federal Home Loan Bank advances or other borrowings if necessary. The strategic focus will continue to be on capitalizing on market conditions, particularly in commercial real estate and multi-family real estate loans, while managing liquidity and capital resources effectively.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Competition: Marathon Bancorp faces significant competition within its market area from a variety of financial institutions, including large banks, community banks, credit unions, and fintech companies. This competition affects both loan origination and deposit attraction, potentially impacting the company's market share and profitability. Additionally, the company's expansion into Southeastern Wisconsin, including the Milwaukee metropolitan area, presents both opportunities and risks as it seeks to grow its commercial real estate and multifamily loan portfolios.
  • Dependency on Borrowings: The company's reliance on the Federal Home Loan Bank of Chicago for borrowings introduces a dependency risk, as changes in borrowing terms or availability could impact liquidity and operational flexibility. Furthermore, the company's strategy to increase commercial real estate and multifamily real estate loans to manage interest rate risk and increase yield may expose it to higher credit risk, particularly in the event of economic downturns affecting the real estate market.
  • Interest Rate Risk: The company has identified the potential impact of rising interest rates on its adjustable-rate loans, which could increase borrower defaults and affect the marketability of underlying collateral. Additionally, the company's focus on commercial real estate and multifamily loans involves greater credit risk compared to residential loans, as these loans depend on the successful operation and management of the properties.
  • Operational Risks: The company also faces operational risks related to its reliance on Marathon Bank's premises, equipment, and staff, which could limit its operational independence and scalability. The ongoing need to comply with comprehensive regulations from the Federal Reserve Board and the Wisconsin Department of Financial Institutions adds a layer of regulatory risk that could affect business operations and strategic initiatives.
  • Market Risks: Marathon Bancorp is exposed to market risks, particularly interest rate risk, which could affect the company's net interest income and the value of its financial assets and liabilities. The company's strategy to focus on commercial and multifamily real estate loans to increase yield may also expose it to market fluctuations in real estate values and demand. Additionally, the company's investment activities, including holdings in corporate debt securities and mortgage-backed securities, are subject to market risk, which could impact the company's financial performance if market conditions deteriorate.

SEC Filing: Marathon Bancorp, Inc. /MD/ [ MBBC ] - 10-K - Sep. 26, 2025

data.title: COPART INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
COPART INC, a leading player in the vehicle remarketing industry, has released its annual 10-K report, detailing significant financial growth, strategic initiatives, and operational highlights for the fiscal year 2025. The report underscores the company's robust performance in both domestic and international markets, alongside its strategic focus on expanding its global footprint and enhancing service offerings.

Financial Highlights

Total Service Revenues and Vehicle Sales: COPART INC reported total service revenues and vehicle sales of $4,646,958 million, marking an 11.4% increase driven by higher revenue per car and increased volume in both U.S. and international markets.

Operating Income: Operating income constituted 36% of total service revenues and vehicle sales, indicating a slight decrease from the previous year due to increased operating expenses.

Net Income: Net income was 33% of total service revenues and vehicle sales, showing a stable performance with a slight decrease from the previous year.

Business Highlights

Geographical Performance: COPART INC operates in multiple regions including the U.S., U.K., Germany, Brazil, Canada, U.A.E., Spain, Finland, Oman, Republic of Ireland, and Bahrain. The U.S. segment generated 83% of the revenue, while the international segment contributed 17%. The company has expanded its operations with new facilities in the U.S., U.K., Spain, and Canada.

Sales Units: The company processed a significant number of vehicles, with 81% of the total number of vehicles processed in fiscal 2025 coming from insurance company sellers. The sales process is facilitated through the VB3 auction platform, which allows for global participation.

New Production Launches: In fiscal 2025, COPART INC opened new operational facilities in the U.K., Spain, and the U.S., enhancing its capacity to process and sell vehicles. This expansion is part of the company's strategy to increase its market presence and operational efficiency.

Service Offerings: The company provides a comprehensive range of services including vehicle remarketing, transportation, title processing, and online auction services. The VB3 platform supports a two-step bidding process, enhancing the competitive bidding environment.

Environmental Stewardship: COPART INC emphasizes its role in reducing the environmental impact of the automotive industry by facilitating the reuse and recycling of vehicles and parts, thus extending the useful life of vehicles and reducing the need for new manufacturing.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue expanding its global footprint by acquiring and developing new facilities, pursuing global and regional vehicle seller agreements, and enhancing its service offerings. The focus remains on increasing revenues and profitability through strategic growth initiatives.

Strategic Initiatives

Expansion Strategy: COPART INC is focused on expanding its global footprint by acquiring and developing new facilities in key markets, including the United States, United Kingdom, Germany, Brazil, Canada, and other international locations. This expansion strategy aims to enhance service offerings and increase market coverage.

Technology and Infrastructure Investments: The company is investing in technology enhancements and infrastructure to support disaster recovery efforts and improve operational efficiency.

Capital Management: COPART INC has a revolving loan facility of $1,250 million, which remains undrawn as of July 31, 2025, providing significant liquidity for future needs. The company has not repurchased any shares under its stock repurchase program during fiscal years 2025, 2024, and 2023, with 325,803,208 shares still available for repurchase. The company has not paid dividends since becoming public and intends to retain earnings for business use. Cash, cash equivalents, and restricted cash increased significantly, driven by strong operating cash flows and proceeds from stock option exercises.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its expansion strategy by acquiring or developing additional locations and expanding current facilities. It anticipates using cash flows from operations and potentially drawing on its revolving loan facility or issuing additional equity to fund this growth. The company also intends to permanently reinvest foreign cash holdings outside the U.S. and does not plan to repatriate these funds for U.S. operations. The focus remains on sustainable growth and enhancing service capabilities globally.

Challenges and Risks

Market Risks: COPART INC is exposed to fluctuations in the market value of salvage and used vehicles, commodity prices, and foreign exchange rates. These factors can affect revenue and profitability, especially given the company's international operations.

Operational Risks: The reliance on a limited number of major vehicle sellers for a substantial portion of revenues poses a risk if these relationships are disrupted. Additionally, the company's expansion into new markets requires successful integration of acquisitions and adaptation to local business practices.

Regulatory Risks: Compliance with a variety of domestic and international laws, including privacy and data protection regulations, is critical. Changes in laws affecting vehicle import/export, environmental regulations, and tax laws could adversely affect operations.

Emerging Risks: The company is increasingly reliant on technology, making it vulnerable to cyber-attacks and data breaches. The implementation of new technologies, such as artificial intelligence, presents both opportunities and regulatory challenges.

Environmental Risks: The operation of storage facilities involves environmental risks, including potential liabilities from contamination and increased regulatory scrutiny related to climate change.

Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by international expansion and the need to manage growth effectively. Strategies include enhancing operational efficiencies, expanding service offerings, and leveraging technology to improve the auction process. The company is also focused on maintaining strong relationships with key vehicle sellers and buyers to mitigate market risks.

SEC Filing: COPART INC [ CPRT ] - 10-K - Sep. 26, 2025

("37 Capital" or the "Company") proposes to conduct a non-brokered private placement to raise up to $375,000 by the issuance of up to 3,000,000 units of the Company at a price of $0.125 per unit. Each unit will consist of one common share of the Company and one share purchase warrant to acquire one common share of the Company at a price of $0.15 per share for a period of three (3) years. If, anytime after six months from the issuance date, in the event that the Company's shares trade on the CSE at $0.35 per share or above for a period of 10 consecutive trading days a, a forced exercise provision will come into effect for the warrants issued in connection with this financing.

Finder's fees may be payable in respect to the above proposed financing and certain insiders may participate in the financing.

The funds raised from the financing will be used towards general working capital as the Company is actively exploring opportunities.

All securities that may be issued in connection with the above transactions will be subject to a four-month and a day hold period and other applicable restrictions under securities laws.

The Company also announces the granting of a total of 2,065,000 incentive stock options ("Options") to directors and officers exercisable at the price of $0.12 per common share for a period of three years. These Options have been reserved for issuance pursuant to the Company's 20% Rolling Stock Option Plan, which has received shareholder approval, subject to certain vesting period. Any shares issued pursuant to the exercise of the Options will be subject to a hold period expiring on January 27, 2026.

data.title: Trump Is Adding More Tariffs. Will They Spur U.S. Production? — WSJ
data.description:
By Chao Deng

President Trump gave a specific rationale for his move this week to hike tariffs on a host of products: to protect U.S. manufacturers from a flood of imports.

Whether the tariffs will help achieve this is an open question, according to economists. Some wondered whether the tariff levels are high enough to stanch the flow of foreign goods, while others pointed out that the duties come with a host of exceptions.

Trump on Thursday evening said he planned a 100% tariff on any branded or patented pharmaceutical product unless the company is building a plant in the U.S. Heavy trucks brought from outside the U.S. would be hit with a 25% tariff, he said, and 50% levies would be added to certain household furnishings.

In theory the notion makes sense: tariffs make goods from abroad more expensive for U.S. consumers, and rising prices should suppress consumer demand and make it easier for domestic producers to compete. This has been a leading theory underpinning Trump's sweeping tariff moves throughout the year.

But some economists point to those moves and question whether they have done what they are intended to do: bring back manufacturing jobs, which have largely moved overseas in recent decades. They say that the effectiveness of Trump's tariffs has been diluted in part by Trump's own willingness to roll back levels initially set against countries like China. The administration has allowed avenues for firms to bypass duties on specific sectors.

If past is prologue, economists say, the sweeping announcements — made on Thursday via three social-media posts containing few details — may not trigger domestic production on the scale Trump would like to see.

"The administration might be able to point to some investments in the U.S. and claim they were due to tariffs," said Neil Shearing, an economist at Capital Economics. "But I don't think they still have a substantial impact on the macroeconomy."

Shearing and others say duties of 50% on kitchen cabinets, bathroom vanities and other similar products probably still aren't high enough to bring production back to the U.S. because of the high cost of American labor, they say. (Only 30% will apply to upholstered furniture.)

Duties of 100% on patented drugs, whose production is more capital intensive, are in theory high enough to drive companies to produce here, they say. But many multinationals — from Johnson & Johnson to Pfizer and Merck — already have plants in the U.S. and therefore might qualify for exemptions. Many patented drugs also come from the European Union, which already struck a deal with the U.S. to cap duties on the bloc's goods at 15%.

Meanwhile, the U.S. imports about 78% of its heavy trucks from Mexico and 15% from Canada. Goods from those two countries that can be shown to largely contain U.S. content are largely exempted from tariffs altogether, under a treaty made during the first Trump administration known as USMCA. So far, many imported cars and auto parts from Mexico and Canada appear to be exempt from the 50% duties under USMCA. (However, steel and aluminum from those countries remain subject to 25% duties, a legacy of Trump's earlier metal tariffs.)

"Because of these exemptions, the tariffs are going to get watered down, " said Ryan Sweet, an economist at Oxford Economics. "The impact is going to be less than what it appears."

Capital Economics' Shearing says had Trump implemented a 100% tariff rate on all imported drugs, the average tariff rate on all goods might have risen from 18% to 26%, assuming 2024 import levels.

But because Trump's tariffs don't apply to generics, the overall tariff bump likely won't be that big. Generics account for only 10% of drug spending, but make up 90% of U.S. drugs consumption by volume.

Exemptions for firms setting up facilities in the U.S. could be even more significant, Shearing adds. And even if the tariff levels were high enough to alter the flow of foreign goods, such effects likely would take a while to show up, given that importers stocked up on significant inventory earlier this year to get ahead of potential tariffs.

The new tariffs could still be a painful adjustment for some firms. Many patented drugs can't be substituted with generics for example, so the cost of many tariffs in that sector will be borne heavily by American hospitals, insurers and patients.

Some economists think the net result of tariffs will achieve the opposite of what Trump wants. Manufacturing's share of U.S. GDP as of June fell to 9.4%, compared with 9.7% at the end of 2024, according to data released Thursday by the Commerce Department's Bureau of Economic Analysis.

Domestic manufacturers that need raw materials are now facing higher prices on an array of parts, from metals to electronics, largely due to the effect of tariffs. Steel for example now costs $960 per ton in the U.S., compared with a world average of $440, according to the U.S. Commerce Department. For some goods, switching to domestic suppliers sometimes isn't possible.

Manufacturing as a share of U.S. GDP has been on the decline for decades. But Ed Gresser, director for trade and global markets at the Progressive Policy Institute, said, "it may be accelerating now because you're adding so many costs to making things in the U.S."

Write to Chao Deng at chao.deng@wsj.com

data.title: Oracle, Caterpillar, and 10 Other Winners from the 'New Global Trade Order' — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Teresa Rivas

Change is the only constant, and 2025 has provided ample evidence of that truth. Yet it's also an opportunity for companies that can take advantage of the shifting tides of the global economy.

Between zigzagging tariffs, rapid pivots in geopolitics, and questions about the future of the Federal Reserve's independence, there has been no shortage of unprecedented and alarming policies in President Donald Trump's second term. The market doesn't seem to mind, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite hitting record highs again at the start of this week, but it is eroding confidence and upending the global paradigm.

The upshot is that even if U.S. markets as a whole keep climbing — thanks to catalysts like artificial intelligence and economic growth — indvidiual companies have to navigate a seemingly constantly changing landscape. Fresh tariffs announced Thursday on pharmaceuticals, building materials and trucks are just the latest example of ongoing uncertainty.

Guessing what could be next is a difficult game, but "what is certain to us is that a new global trade order is taking shape," writes Société Générale Equity Strategist Makhdoom Muteeb Raina.

There are real world constraints on what the administration can do in its quest to remake global trade, ranging from legal challenges to the logistics of undoing complex manufacturing systems, and China's own influence. Nonetheless, there is likely to be some degree of shifting of supply chains as well as "decoupling from China in respect of strategic goods," as the firm puts it.

That means investors should be looking to identify winners and losers in the new normal.

"Leaders include companies benefiting i) from an increase in manufacturing capex, ii) a focus on sovereignty, and iii) high local content in the U.S.," writes Muteeb Raina. "Conversely, laggards include companies hit by i) efficiency loss, ii) increased competition, and iii) a decline in exports."

SocGen established investment buckets identifying leaders and laggards in June, and since then the framework is still intact, he writes.

The dozen stocks that were rated highest on that list were Oracle, Linde, Caterpillar, Honeywell International, Palo Alto Networks, Eaton Corp., Prologis, Fortinet, Air Products & Chemicals, Cloudflare, Freeport-McMoRan, and Quanta Services.

At the time, Apple and Amazon.com topped the laggards list, given tariff exposure, which has been somewhat resolved. The other top 10 names were McDonald's, Starbucks, PayPal Holdings, GM, Target, Constellation Brands, Stellantis, Corpay, Zoom Communications, and Dollar Tree.

How global trade ultimately shakes out remains an open question, but it does seem that investors can count on things not going back to the way they were.

Write to Teresa Rivas at teresa.rivas@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Honeywell Boosts Quarterly Dividend by 5.3%
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Honeywell it has raised its quarterly dividend 5.3% to $1.19 a share, from $1.13.

The new payout, equal to $4.76 a year, represents an annual yield of about 2.3% based on Friday's closing price of $208.15.

The increase reflects Honeywell's focus on value creation and its confidence in the future, the company said Friday.

Honeywell said it has now raised its annual dividend 16 times in the past 15 years.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: COP30 urged to link climate justice with reparations for historical crimes
data.description:
By Catarina Demony

Hundreds of environmental and human rights groups have urged COP30, the global climate summit to be held in Brazil this November, to confront the historical roots of the climate crisis, and put reparations on the agenda.

The letter argues that historical crimes, such as enslavement and colonialism, created global inequalities in resource access, drove asymmetrical contributions to emissions, increased vulnerability to disasters, and continue to exclude many from climate decision-making.

"There can be no true climate justice without reparatory justice," the signatories said.

Launched by organisations such as Brazil's Instituto Luiz Gama and the Caribbean Pan African Network earlier this week, it has now been signed by more than 240 organisations and public figures, including Amnesty International USA and local chapters of Black Lives Matter.

Colombia's environment minister has also backed the letter. It will be sent to the Brazilian government and the United Nations next week.

"The climate crisis did not arise recently — it is a continuation of centuries of greenhouse emissions, extraction, dispossession, and racial violence," the letter said, urging COP30 to address historical injustices and the need for reparations as part of any negotiation on climate.

The letter said that it would be significant for Brazil, which received more enslaved people than any other country and today has the largest population of African descendants outside Africa, to take the lead on this call.

It urged Brazil and COP30 to create an official space at the summit dedicated to climate justice and reparations, led by Africans, people of African descent, and Indigenous communities.

It also asked Brazil to incorporate these themes into its political agenda and actively facilitate such conversations at COP30.

Centuries-old demands for reparations have been gaining renewed momentum worldwide but the backlash against them has also been growing, with critics saying modern states and institutions should not pay or make other amends for historical wrongs.

Anielle Franco, Brazil's minister of racial equality, told Reuters last year the wrongs of the past had long gone unrecognised by those in power, and that reparations were about "building a more dignified future."

data.title: US labor board withdraws claims Apple CEO violated employee rights, Bloomberg News reports
data.description: The U.S. labor board has withdrawn its allegations that Apple CEO Tim Cook violated federal labor law, Bloomberg News reported on Friday citing a letter it had seen.

data.title: UK to offer to pay more for some drugs to appease Trump, pharma groups, FT reports
data.description:
The UK government is set to offer to pay more for certain medicines to protect pharmaceutical exports to the United States, The Financial Times reported on Friday, amid threats of 100% tariffs on drugs by Donald Trump's administration.

British Prime Minister Keir Starmer's chief business adviser, Varun Chandra, will travel to Washington next week with a message that Britain is willing to increase its spending on medicines, the report added.

The British government did not immediately respond to Reuters' request for comment. Reuters could not immediately verify the report.

Leading the gains are Boeing (3.67%), Travelers Companies (1.27%) and Merck (1.24%).

Top losers were Cisco Systems (-0.93%), Apple (-0.57%) and Coca-Cola (-0.52%).

data.title: $2.1B Loss, No Heir, and a Fire Sale: Inside New World's Meltdown
data.description:
New World Development Co. (NDVLY) has reported another year of steep losses as Hong Kong's property downturn and mounting debt pressures continue to bite. For the year ended June 30, the company posted a HK$16.3 billion loss from continuing operations, compared with HK$11.8 billion a year earlier, largely driven by impairment provisions and one-time charges. Consolidated net debt edged down slightly to HK$120.1 billion, but management has suspended dividend payments and deferred distributions on perpetual capital securities to preserve liquidity.

CEO Echo Huang emphasized that the group's top priority is to safeguard cash flow and reduce leverage. The company has set a target of HK$27 billion in proceeds from contracted sales and asset disposals by next June. CFO Edward Lau noted there are no immediate plans for rights issues or placements, highlighting instead efforts to strengthen financing through other means. Earlier this year, New World secured an $11 billion refinancing package, followed by a smaller HK$3.95 billion loan. Discussions are also ongoing with investors, including Blackstone, as the company explores potential capital injections.

The operating environment remains highly challenging. Home prices in Hong Kong are still nearly 30% below their 2021 peak, while China's housing downturn has stretched into a fifth year. Commercial property values have fallen even more sharply, with office and retail prices down 48% and 41% respectively from 2018 highs. New World also recognized a HK$2.7 billion impairment on its 11 Skies airport mall project, which could be put up for sale as part of its liquidity strategy. The firm, long viewed as one of Hong Kong's leading developers, now faces additional questions following the sudden departure of heir Adrian Cheng in 2024, raising investor concerns over succession and long-term stability.

data.title: Wall Street Stunned: Trump Slaps $14B Fire Sale Tag on TikTok US
data.description:
The Trump administration has floated a $14 billion valuation for TikTok's US business, a figure that has stunned investors and analysts who had pegged its worth closer to $40 billion. Vice President JD Vance, speaking Thursday, acknowledged that buyers would ultimately decide the price, but his comments reinforced President Donald Trump's push for an American-led acquisition. Oracle and Silver Lake are positioned as potential bidders, and while such a low entry point could look attractive for them, the figure is being called deeply misaligned with the platform's size and earnings power. Ashwin Binwani, founder of Alpha Binwani Capital, said the proposed tag could be the most undervalued tech acquisition of the decade, estimating it reflects only a third of TikTok's real value.

TikTok has become one of the most heavily used social media platforms in the United States, commanding 170 million active users and generating revenue north of $10 billion annually in this market alone. At the suggested $14 billion price, the US unit would be valued at just 1.4 times sales, putting it on par with mature, slower-growth companies such as Exxon Mobil or General Mills. By comparison, Meta's Instagram trades at around 10 times sales and Alphabet's YouTube at roughly eight. Analysts say this gap highlights the disconnect between TikTok's market influence and the floated valuation, with some describing it as daylight robbery.

The plan would require TikTok's US operations to be spun into a joint venture, with ByteDance's stake cut below 20% to satisfy Washington's national security demands. Trump has said Xi Jinping has offered his blessing, though Beijing has not confirmed this publicly, leaving the geopolitical dimension unresolved. Questions also remain about governance, since none of the anticipated buyers are consumer-facing internet operators. As Alvin Foo, a venture partner at Zero2Launch, put it, the deal resembles sell or stop, underscoring the pressure on ByteDance as it faces the 120-day deadline.

data.title: U.S. to Honor 15% Tariff Rate on EU Pharmaceuticals, Official Says — WSJ
data.description:
By Meridith McGraw

All of the tariffs Trump announced late Thursday-on pharmaceuticals, big trucks and certain home goods-were expected to fall under a provision of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962 that allows presidents to impose trade restrictions such as tariffs as part of national-security decisions, according to an administration official.

The official added that the 100% pharmaceutical tariffs would apply on a product-by-product basis under a process to be set up by the Commerce Department. This official said the U.S. would honor the EU trade agreement capping pharmaceutical tariffs at 15%, and a deal with Japan that set Trump's so-called reciprocal tariffs at 15% for the country.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Cava Group Inc.: Jonathan Braatvedt to Serve as Interim Head of Operations >CAVA
Cava Group Inc. Announces Departure of Chief Operations Officer Jennifer Somers >CAVA
SC Jobs Economic Authority Offering $113 Million in Senior Living Revenue Bonds
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis

The South Carolina Jobs Economic Authority is offering $113 million in senior living revenue bonds, with proceeds from the offering slated to finance acquisition, renovation and construction of three senior living facilities in the state.

Proceeds from the offering will be loaned to Connexion Communities Simpsonville, Connexion Communities Travelers Rest, and Connexion Communities Greenville, three South Carolina liability companies. The companies will use the funds to acquire, build and equip a 174-unit senior living care facility, as well as a pair of 66-unit senior living communities.

The bonds will be backed by loan payments, as well as by a debt service reserve fund, according to documents on MuniOS.

The offering will consist of $112.5 million of Series 2025 A-1 senior living revenue bonds, which will be sold in three tranches maturing on Oct. 1, 2035, Oct. 1, 2045 and Oct. 1, 2060. Interest from these bonds is exempt from federal and South Carolina state taxes. The offering also contains $855,000 of federally taxable Series 2025 A-2 bonds, which haven't yet received a maturity date. Interest and yield on the offerings hasn't been set.

The bonds are unrated, and no ratings have been requested.

The South Carolina Jobs Economic Authority acts as a statewide conduit issuer of special obligation revenue bonds and assists borrowers in accessing financial markets and capital, it said.

DA Davidson is listed as the offering's manager.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com

data.title: Lennar Corporation Declares Quarterly Dividends
data.description:
MIAMI, Sept. 26, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Lennar Corporation ( and LEN.B), one of the nation's leading homebuilders, announced that its Board of Directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.50 per share for both Class A and Class B common stock payable on October 27, 2025 to holders of record at the close of business on October 10, 2025.

About Lennar

Lennar Corporation, founded in 1954, is one of the nation's leading builders of quality homes for all generations. Lennar builds affordable, move-up and active adult homes primarily under the Lennar brand name. Lennar's Financial Services segment provides mortgage financing, title and closing services primarily for buyers of Lennar's homes and, through LMF Commercial, originates mortgage loans secured primarily by commercial real estate properties throughout the United States. Lennar's Multifamily segment is a nationwide developer of high-quality multifamily rental properties. LEN(X) drives Lennar's technology, innovation and strategic investments. For more information about Lennar, please visit www.lennar.com.

Contact:

Ian Frazer

Investor Relations

Lennar Corporation

(305) 485-4129

SOURCE Lennar Corporation
